name: Google Maps Platform
what_it_does: Provides REST APIs for geocoding addresses, computing directions and routes, searching places, rendering static map images, and embedding interactive maps in applications.
use_cases: Geocode a user-supplied address to lat/lng coordinates for storage or display
Compute turn-by-turn driving, cycling, or transit directions between two points
Search for nearby businesses or points of interest using Places API text or nearby search
Render a static map image thumbnail of a location for embedding in emails or reports
Validate and autocomplete partial address strings in real-time forms using Places Autocomplete
not_for: Offline or air-gapped map rendering without internet access to Google servers
Bulk geocoding millions of addresses cheaply — costs add up quickly beyond free credit
Open-source or self-hosted map tile serving where vendor lock-in is a concern
best_when: You need accurate geocoding, routing, and places data backed by Google's global dataset with a well-documented REST API and official SDKs.
avoid_when: Your workload exceeds the $200/month free credit and budget is constrained, or you need open-source infrastructure free of Google's terms of service.

auth.notes: Single API key passed as query parameter `key=`. Keys can be restricted by HTTP referrer, IP address, or specific APIs via the Cloud Console. Unrestricted keys are a common security risk.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $200/month free credit (~40,000 geocoding requests or ~28,000 directions requests)
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-as-you-go per API call after credit exhausted; e.g., Geocoding $5/1000, Directions $10/1000, Places Nearby $32/1000
pricing.notes: Billing account and credit card required to enable APIs even within free tier. Set budget alerts to avoid surprise charges.

performance.rate_limits: Geocoding: 50 QPS per project; Directions: 50 QPS; Places: 100 QPS. Monthly quota caps apply per API.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTP status codes with JSON `error_message` field and `status` string (e.g., ZERO_RESULTS, OVER_QUERY_LIMIT) are clear and actionable for agents.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are GET-based read operations; safe to retry without side effects.
known_agent_gotchas: API key passed as a query parameter can be logged in server access logs — use environment variables and restrict key permissions
OVER_QUERY_LIMIT errors require exponential backoff; aggressive retry loops will rapidly exhaust quota
Places API Next Page Token has a mandatory 2-second delay before it becomes valid — polling immediately returns INVALID_REQUEST
Geocoding returns multiple results ordered by relevance; agents must handle ambiguous results rather than assuming index 0 is always correct
Static Maps URL length is capped at 8192 characters; complex polyline overlays may need encoding or splitting

security_score_components.security_notes: API key in query string is a well-known exposure risk. Key restrictions (IP, referrer, API) partially mitigate this. No OAuth or per-token scoping available for server-side use.
